Uses of Class
recoder.ModelException

Packages that use ModelException
Package
Description
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.recoderext
 
recoder
This package contains some basic definitions.
recoder.abstraction
This package contains the meta model abstractions as used by the semantical services.
recoder.bytecode
This package contains classes that cover information derived from Java byte code.
recoder.java
This package contains classes that cover the Java programming language.
recoder.java.declaration
Elements of the Java syntax tree representing declarations.
recoder.kit.pattern
This package contains classes that model design patterns; it is far from being stable and will be subject to changes.
recoder.service
This package contains the semantical services of RECODER.

    JavaProgramElement.validate()

    Description
    class 
    InconsistentPatternException
     
    Methods in recoder.kit.pattern that throw ModelException
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    Factory.validate()
    Checks all factory methods and ensures that all methods are members of the same type.
    void
    FactoryMethod.validate()
    The validation ensures that a producer exists and that the return value of the producer matches the product.
    void
    Property.validate()
    Checks that at least one getter or one setter is available and that the types of the participants match.

    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    AmbiguousDeclarationException
    Exception indicating that a particular declaration is ambiguous.
    class 
    AmbiguousImportException
    Exception indicating that a particular import is ambiguous.
    class 
    AmbiguousReferenceException
    Exception indicating that a particular reference is ambiguous.
    class 
    AmbiguousStaticFieldImportException
    Exception indicating that two static imports find fields with the same name.
    class 
    CyclicInheritanceException
    Exception indicating a cyclic inheritance.
    class 
    IllegalModifierException
     
    class 
    MissingClassFileException
    Exception indicating that a vital class file is missing, usually a super type of a known class file.
    class 
    NoSuchTransformationException
    Exception indicating that a transformation is not accessible.
    class 
    TypeImportException
    Exception indicating that a certain type could not be imported.
    class 
    TypingException
    Exception indicating that a particular type rule has been violated.
    class 
    UnresolvedReferenceException
    Exception indicating that a particular reference (or reference prefix) could not be resolved.
